message:
feat(indexer): add mode-aware index-run lock, fix watch-once state isolation, and harden watcher error handling

Index-run lock now carries mode metadata:
- Introduce IndexRunMode enum (Index, WatchStartup, Watch, WatchOnce)
  that is written into the lock file alongside pid/started_at_ms/db_path.
  This lets `cass status` (via state_meta_json) distinguish a daemon in
  steady-state watch mode from an active rebuild, preventing the UI from
  reporting an idle watcher as "perpetually rebuilding."
- IndexRunLockGuard gains a set_mode() method so the indexer can
  transition from WatchStartup -> Watch after the initial rebuild
  completes, updating the lock file in place.
- Lock acquisition now distinguishes WouldBlock (another process holds
  the lock) from other IO errors, giving clearer error messages instead
  of a generic "failed to acquire" context.

Watch-once state isolation:
- reindex_paths() now skips advancing the persistent watch_state
  high-water marks when processing explicit watch-once imports. These
  are one-shot recovery/replay operations, not live daemon progress.
  Without this fix, a watch-once replay of old sessions would advance
  the timestamp and cause steady-state watch mode to skip legitimately
  new sessions.
- New test: reindex_paths_watch_once_does_not_advance_persistent_watch_state
  verifies both in-memory and on-disk watch state remain unchanged.

force_rebuild correctness:
- When force_rebuild is set, the indexer now skips resuming a stale
  lexical rebuild checkpoint and avoids the canonical-only optimization
  path. Previously, force_rebuild could resume a partially completed
  rebuild from a prior run, defeating its purpose.

Filesystem watcher hardening:
- The notify callback now logs watcher errors via tracing::warn instead
  of silently discarding them. This makes it possible to diagnose inotify
  limit exhaustion, permission issues, or other OS-level watch failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
